terms.acceptTermsMessage: Check the box to accept terms for this lot, then click Next Lot.
terms.allTermsAcceptedMessage: You've accepted terms for all lots. Click Participate to continue.

prebid.placeFirstPrebidMessage: Enter your pre-bid amount for this lot. This is the price at which you're willing to accept.
prebid.goToNextLot: Go to the next lot and place your pre-bid.
prebid.allPrebidsPlacedMessage: You can now start the training, or lower your pre-bids before the auction begins.

training.startTrainingMessage: Click to start the training. All lots will run according to the configured timing.
training.trainingInProgressMessage: The auction is running. Lots will auto-advance based on the timing rule.
training.trainingCompleteMessage: Click Reset to try different scenarios or adjust your pre-bids.

nextLot.goToNextLotMessage: Click to navigate to this lot and place your pre-bid.

dutch.first: Finish at rank #1 with a pre-bid
dutch.second: Finish at rank #1 with a live bid

dutch-preferred.first: Finish at rank #1 with a pre-bid
dutch-preferred.second: Finish at rank #1 with a live bid

demoItemSharedText.description: To better understand the eAuction, go through {scenarioCount} scenarios that simulate different possible outcomes:

demoItem.title: Sealed bid eAuction
demoItem.description: A hidden-bid event where participants submit their best offers without live ranking.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: A hidden-bid event where participants submit their best offers without live ranking.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**During the event:** Participants place bids. Bids remain undisclosed; participants may lower and resubmit their offer any number of times.

**End of the event:** Ranks are disclosed depending on final submitted offers.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: At the end of the eAuction, each supplier receives a final rank. The lot is awarded according to the final ranking and the eAuction’s awarding principles.
howToWinPanel.description: *(eg. For example, if a supplier finishes in Rank #1 and the awarding principles specify that the top-ranked supplier is awarded 70% of the lot volume, then that supplier will be granted 70% of the volume.)*

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: The eAuction runs for a fixed duration. Once the countdown reaches zero, the eAuction ends and participants are informed of their ranks.

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: A transformation means that **bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors** such as quality, logistics, or delivery.
handicapPanel.description: These adjustments, called **handicaps**, can be either **dynamic** (applied once at the start) or **fix** (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different outcome at the end of the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: Rank #1: You are ranked #1 at the end of the eAuction.
scenarioPanel.secondScenario: Rank #2  You are ranked #2 or less at the end of the eAuction.

demoItem.title: English eAuction
demoItem.description: A real-time competitive event with live ranking after an initial pre-bid.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: A real-time competitive event with live ranking after an initial pre-bid.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**Before the event:** Participants must submit a pre-bid before the start of the event.

**During the event:** Participants see live ranking and can improve their offers in real time.

**End of the event:** Ranks are defined depending on final submitted offers.

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: At the end of the eAuction, each supplier receives a final rank. The lot is awarded according to the final ranking and the eAuction’s awarding principles.
howToWinPanel.description: *(eg. For example, if a supplier finishes in Rank #1 and the awarding principles specify that the top-ranked supplier is awarded 70% of the lot volume, then that supplier will be granted 70% of the volume.)*

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: Adjusts bids based on buyer's evaluation criteria, giving participants fixed or dynamic advantages to ensure qualitative comparison between offers.

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: Runs for a limited time with the possibility of an **overtime** period. The eAuction ends when the timer reaches zero.
timingPanel.description: **Overtime:** If a bid is placed during the final minutes (e.g., last 1, 2, or 3 minutes), the timer resets to the corresponding overtime value.

prebidPanel.title: ⚙️ Option : Prebid
prebidPanel.subtitle: Initial offer submitted before the eAuction begins. Defines the supplier starting rank at the beginning of the eAuction.

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different outcome at the end of the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: Rank #1: You are ranked #1 at the end of the eAuction.
scenarioPanel.secondScenario: Rank #2  You are ranked #2 or less at the end of the eAuction.

demoItem.title: Dutch eAuction
demoItem.description: A first-accepts format where price rises in steps until someone accepts.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: A first-accepts format where price rises in steps until someone accepts.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**Before the event:** Participants may submit a pre-bid before the start of the event.

**During the event:** The price increases round by round in fixed increments; no live ranking is shown.

**End of the event:** The first participant to accept the current price ends the event and secures the lot. Other participants are informed of the end of the lot.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: The first participant to accept a price ends the eAuction and is ranked #1. All other participants are ranked #2. The lot is awarded according to the final ranking and the eAuction’s awarding principles.

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: A transformation means that **bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors** such as quality, logistics, or delivery.
handicapPanel.description: These adjustments, called **handicaps**, can be either **dynamic** (applied once at the start) or **fix** (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: Runs in fixed-duration **rounds** within a defined overall time period.
timingPanel.description: **Rounds:** Fixed time intervals, each with an associated price that changes from one round to the next. The price increases at each interval by a fixed and predefined increment.

prebidPanel.title: ⚙️ Option : Prebid
prebidPanel.subtitle: Offer placed before the eAuction begins. Sets the price level at which the system will automatically place the bid for the participant.
prebidPanel.description: Priority will be given to participant placing first a pre-bid, and then to participants placing a live bid.

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different outcome at the end of the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: Rank #1 with pre-bid: You placed a pre-bid, and no other participants accepted a price before your pre-bid was reached. You end the eAuction ranked #1.
scenarioPanel.secondScenario: Rank #1 with live bid: You placed a pre-bid, but before the eAuction reached your price, you submitted a live bid by clicking “Place a bid”. You end the eAuction ranked #1.
scenarioPanel.thirdScenario: Rank #2: Another participants accepted a price before you. The eAuction ends, and you are ranked #2.
scenarioPanel.fourthScenario: Rank #1: You accepted a price level by clicking “Place a bid”. You end the eAuction ranked #1.

demoItem.title: Dutch Preferred eAuction
demoItem.description: A first-accepts format where price rises in steps until someone accepts. Priority access is given to selected participants each round.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: A first-accepts format where price rises in steps until someone accepts. Priority access is given to selected participants each round.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**Before the event:** Participants may submit a pre-bid before the start of the event.

**During the event:** The price increases round by round in fixed increments; no live ranking is shown. Selected participants receive priority access to each new round before others.

**End of the event:** The first participant to accept the current price ends the event and secures the lot. Other participants are informed of the end of the lot.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: The first participant to accept a price ends the eAuction and is ranked #1. All other participants are ranked #2. The lot is awarded according to the final ranking and the eAuction’s awarding principles.

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: A transformation means that **bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors** such as quality, logistics, or delivery.
handicapPanel.description: These adjustments, called **handicaps**, can be either **dynamic** (applied once at the start) or **fix** (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: Runs in fixed-duration **rounds** within a defined overall time period but with different access periods. Preferred participants get early access while other must wait.
timingPanel.description: **Rounds:** Fixed time intervals, each with an associated price that changes from one round to the next. The price increases at each interval by a fixed and predefined increment.

prebidPanel.title: ⚙️ Option : Prebid
prebidPanel.subtitle:
Offer placed before the eAuction begins. Sets the price level at which the system will automatically place the bid for the participant.

Priority will be given to participant placing first a pre-bid, and then to participants placing a live bid.

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different outcome at the end of the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: Rank #1 with pre-bid: You placed a pre-bid, and no other participants accepted a price before your pre-bid was reached. You end the eAuction ranked #1.
scenarioPanel.secondScenario: Rank #1 with live bid: You placed a pre-bid, but before the eAuction reached your price, you submitted a live bid by clicking “Place a bid”. You end the eAuction ranked #1.
scenarioPanel.thirdScenario: Rank #2: Another participants accepted a price before you. The eAuction ends, and you are ranked #2.
scenarioPanel.fourthScenario: Rank #1: You accepted a price level by clicking “Place a bid”. You end the eAuction ranked #1.

demoItem.title: Japanese eAuction
demoItem.description: An elimination format where price drops in steps and exits are final.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: An elimination format where price drops in steps and exits are final.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**Before the event:** Participants may submit a pre-bid before the start of the event.

**During the event:** The price decreases round by round; participants must accept each new price to stay active; no live ranking is shown. Exits are final. 

**End of the event:** Upon exit, each participant is informed of their final rank.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: When a supplier exits the eAuction, he receives his final rank. The lot is awarded according to the final ranking and the eAuction’s awarding principles.
howToWinPanel.description: *(eg. For example, if a supplier finishes in Rank #1 and the awarding principles specify that the top-ranked supplier is awarded 70% of the lot volume, then that supplier will be granted 70% of the volume.)*

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: A transformation means that **bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors** such as quality, logistics, or delivery.
handicapPanel.description: These adjustments, called **handicaps**, can be either **dynamic** (applied once at the start) or **fix** (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: Runs in fixed-duration **rounds** within a defined overall time period.
timingPanel.description: **Rounds:** Fixed time intervals, each with an associated price that changes from one round to the next. The price decreases at each interval by a fixed and predefined increment.

prebidPanel.title: ⚙️ Option : Prebid
prebidPanel.subtitle: Offer placed before the eAuction begins. The system automatically accepts each round until the price reaches the supplier’s pre-bid level

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different outcome at the end of the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: Rank #1: You are ranked #1 at the end of the eAuction.
scenarioPanel.secondScenario: Rank #2  You are ranked #2 or less at the end of the eAuction.

demoItem.title: Japanese No Rank eAuction
demoItem.description: A price discovery format with elimination rounds and no automatic award at the end of the event.

howItWorkPanel.title: 📘 Definition
howItWorkPanel.subtitle: A price discovery format with elimination rounds and no automatic award at the end of the event.
howItWorkPanel.description:
**Before the event:** Participants may submit a pre-bid before the start of the event.

**During the event:** The price decreases round by round; participants must accept each new price to stay active; no live ranking is shown. Exits are final. 

**End of the event:** The buyer may decide later to award the lot at that final price, with no renegotiation of price after the event.

howToWinPanel.title: 🎯 Awarding
howToWinPanel.subtitle: The lot is awarded outside the eAuction based on the supplier’s final price and the buyer’s evaluation criterias.

multilotPanel.title: 🎰 Option: Multi-lot
multilotPanel.subtitle: Indicates that the eAuction includes several lots.
multilotPanel.description: Each lot represents a specific item or product and is treated as an independent event with its own ranking, parameters, and awarding rules.

handicapPanel.title: 🔁 Option : Transformation
handicapPanel.subtitle: A transformation means that **bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors** such as quality, logistics, or delivery.
handicapPanel.description: These adjustments, called **handicaps**, can be either **dynamic** (applied once at the start) or **fix** (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

timingPanel.title: ⏱️ Timing
timingPanel.subtitle: Runs in fixed-duration **rounds** within a defined overall time period.
timingPanel.description: **Rounds:** Fixed time intervals, each with an associated price that changes from one round to the next. The price decreases at each interval by a fixed and predefined increment.

prebidPanel.title: ⚙️ Option : Prebid
prebidPanel.subtitle: Offer placed before the eAuction begins. The system automatically accepts each round until the price reaches the supplier’s pre-bid level

scenarioPanel.title: 💡 {scenarioCount} different scenarios for the eAuction
scenarioPanel.firstScenario: You exit the eAuction at the price level that suits you; either at your pre-bid level or later by staying active for additional rounds.
